programmes.show_description: Anything Short of a Blow to the Head: Ah Sahm and Young Jun receive advice about producing authentic bills. The Secret Service tracks counterfeit money. (S3, ep 2 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 1)
Pie and Sauce: The driver of his car is spoken to by his lack of plates, a man tests positive for alcohol and meth use and Highway Patrol's biggest fan is pulled over. (S12, ep 5)
Big Trouble: Officers pull over a duo who have swapped seats to skirt the law, a bright yellow car highlights a dangerous driver and a party-goer has his plans put on hold. (S12, ep 6)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 9)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 10)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 3)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 4)
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A man walking on the motorway inadvertently leads police straight to a bust and a sheep runs amok. (S16, ep 1)
The police get way more than they bargain for when they pursue a fleeing driver, then meet one of the drunkest men you'll ever see. (S16, ep 2)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a Japanese traveller must make a difficult decision. An officer discovers something seedy at the mail facility in Chicago. (S1, ep 28)
A hostile traveller spits out her true intentions, while a farmer's choice of crop makes him inadmissible. Plus, mail room officers smell a rat. (S2, ep 1)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists.  (S4, ep 11)

Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 7)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 8)
An officer suspects a US resident returning from the Caribbean may be hiding something and two construction workers wind up at the border by mistake. (S1, ep 19)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a pregnant Chinese national has CBP asking questions and a security breach on the tarmac leads officers to a jet bound for Haiti. (S1, ep 20)
Immigration deal with a Singaporean national who claims to be meeting a mystery man. Plus, a flood in the biosecurity zone shuts down the airport. (S11, ep 5)
Documentary series following the men and woman who work to keep New Zealand's borders safe. (S11, ep 6)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary series following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists. (S3, ep 20)

Second Sight: Sisko falls in love for the first time since his wife's death, but the object of his affections may not be all that she seems.
Window of Opportunity: During one of the funniest episodes ever made, Jack O'Neill and Teal'c relive the same day over and over after being exposed to a strange alien device. (S4, ep 6)
Human Error: Seven of Nine tries to perfect her social skills on the holodeck, but her hobby becomes an obsession and she starts to neglect her duties. (S7, ep 18)
Hide and Q: The Enterprise is once again challenged by the dangerous and powerful 'Q', who offers Riker god-like powers in exchange for Riker's membership in the 'Q' continuum.
A nervous man claims he has arrived to visit his wife for their wedding anniversary but what's in his luggage may spell the end of his trip. A drug delivery is intercepted. (S15, ep 7)
A man from Amsterdam says he's here for a holiday, but his suspicious travel itinerary through China and an empty suitcase seem to tell a very different story. (S15, ep 8)
Steven Dean Gordon: After Jarrae Estepp's body was found at a recycling centre, police discovered that her murderer, Steven Dean Gordon, had killed before. (S8, ep 10)
Peter Wrighton: The story of the senseless killing of 83-year-old Peter Wrighton, who was targeted by Alexander Palmer while out walking his dog in 2017.
Strange New World: On an Earth-like planet, the crew become infected with powerful hallucinogens. Can Archer save them before the ensuing paranoia causes a tragedy?
No Time For F*cking Chemistry: While taking menacing steps to secure Nellie's land, Strickland instils confidence in Leary's political abilities. (S3, ep 3 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 2)
Bourbon Blues: Officers pull over a suspended driver with a box of bourbons by his side, a late night takeaway run turns sour and a gymnastics coach stretches himself thin. (S12, ep 7)
Old But Gold: An elderly woman drives with a suspended license, a young family's day turns from bad to worse and a driver has a terrible burnout. (S12, ep 8)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 1)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 2)

A runaway driver clocks huge speeds as he flees from police, a motorcyclist goes head first through a car window and a man suffers an asthma attack - or does he? (S16, ep 3)
A driver flees a late-night crash scene; a chainsaw-wielding biker attracts police attention; and one of the weirdest breakdowns you'll see. (S16, ep 4)
A street performer is caught in the middle of an escape act, while a cheeky bus passenger answers questions about his mischievous past. (S2, ep 2)
A distinctive bag has officers seeing double, and a photographer misses the big picture. Plus, a heavily-modified truck causes concern. (S2, ep 3)

A Canadian is interviewed under oath after CBP discovers pictures of pot plants on his phone. Without paperwork, it could be curtains for a Venezuelan conductor. (S1, ep 21)
CBP questions a Canadian attempting to enter the US with three of his American family members. An officer has no time for a Swiss traveller's watch declaration. (S1, ep 22)
A Brazilian man hiding a large quantity of cocaine is busted at Auckland Airport. Plus, a woman with undeclared seeds loses her cool with Biosecurity. (S11, ep 7)
Immigration deal with a Chinese national using a fake passport, while custom discover a huge stash of meth hidden among household items. (S11, ep 8)

Sanctuary: Kira finds herself in a dilemma when a displaced alien race arrives on Deep Space Nine and claims Bajor as its people's legendary homeland.
Watergate: When the Russians discover a stargate, they turn to SG-1 for help. (S4, ep 7)
Q2: When Q's son becomes reckless and uncontrollable, Q places him in Captain Janeway's care for a week. Can she transform the teen? (S7, ep 19)
Too Short A Season: The Enterprise escorts a Federation admiral to negotiate the release of hostages. But things take a turn when the crew discovers the planet's governor is after revenge.
Three visitors from Malaysia raise alarms when it emerges that they've cancelled their accommodation just before their flight and their lies begin to unravel. (S15, ep 9)
When a Chinese bulk carrier docks in Newcastle it appears there's been a major security breach and what the Border Force officers find is shocking. (S15, ep 10)
The Murder in the Tower Block: When a body is found in a flat, a trail of forensic evidence allows detectives to distinguish between friend and foe. (S2, ep 10)
The Murder with No Body: The tragic case of 22-year-old Helen McCourt, who got off a bus one night in her hometown but never arrived at her nearby home. (S2, ep 11)
Unexpected: After sharing a friendly moment with the female engineers of an alien ship, Trip discovers that he's just had his first experience with alien sex and is now pregnant!
